nodes.name: assemble_static_mapping_context
nodes.description: Construct immutable algorithm context from constructor inputs so all later stages consume explicit state instead of hidden class fields.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.constraints: must be a valid source graph

outputs.name: mapping_context
outputs.constraints: contains graph, lattice, lattice_instance, previously_generated_subgraphs, seed as immutable fields

nodes.type_signature: (graph: Graph, lattice_instance: LatticeInstance, previously_generated_subgraphs: Collection[Subgraph], seed: int|None) -> MappingContext
nodes.conceptual_summary: ImmutableCompositeStateAssembler. Aggregates multiple externally supplied data structures and an optional deterministic control scalar into a single explicit state record. The transform is structural: it preserves each input by reference/value while reifying them as immutable fields, eliminating implicit ambient state. The result is a canonical context object that downstream procedures can consume with fixed, reproducible access semantics.. Applications: Computational biology: package interaction network, folding-topology constraints, prior motif candidates, and optional randomization seed into a reproducible experiment context, Robotics planning: package environment connectivity map, kinematic constraint manifold, prior trajectory fragments, and tie-break seed into a fixed planning context, Distributed systems optimization: package service dependency graph, resource-topology model, prior allocation subplans, and deterministic seed into an immutable scheduling context, Materials simulation: package atomic connectivity structure, lattice/mesh topology instance, previously sampled local configurations, and seed into a reproducible simulation context

nodes.name: initialize_frontier_from_start_node
nodes.description: Create initial mapping/unmapping frontier state from a selected starting node.

outputs.name: initialized_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: returns new mapping/unmapping/frontier state; no hidden mutation

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode]) -> MappingState
nodes.conceptual_summary: SeedBidirectionalCorrespondenceFrontier. Initializes a search state by anchoring a partial correspondence between two relational structures at a selected source element. It takes an existing forward map, its inverse map, and a set of unexpanded source elements, then returns a new immutable state where correspondence consistency constraints are preserved. The result is a frontier-ready state for subsequent expansion steps in a correspondence-construction process.. Applications: Bootstrapping residue-contact-graph to template-topology alignment in computational biology, Seeding component-netlist to physical-layout correspondence in electronic design automation, Initializing landmark-topology matching between sensed environment graphs and prior maps in robotics, Starting entity-alignment search between heterogeneous knowledge graphs in information systems

nodes.name: ScoreAndExtendGreedyCandidates
nodes.description: Score candidate placements and greedily extend the mapping frontier using ranking and validity filtering flags.

inputs.name: remove_invalid_placement_nodes
inputs.constraints: if true, prune invalid placements early

outputs.name: extended_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: new mapping/unmapping/frontier after greedy extension

outputs.name: candidate_scores
outputs.constraints: deterministic from inputs

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, considered_nodes: List[GraphNode], unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode], free_lattice_neighbors: Set[LatticeNode], mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> tuple[MappingState, Map[GraphNode,float]]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Greedy Frontier-Constrained Correspondence Expansion. Given a partial one-to-one correspondence between two discrete structures, the operation computes deterministic fitness scores for currently eligible source elements relative to feasible target-adjacent slots under local consistency constraints. It can optionally remove infeasible candidates before selection and optionally rank candidates by score. It then performs a greedy state transition that commits high-priority valid correspondences, expanding the boundary of mapped elements while updating both forward and inverse lookup relations.. Applications: Incremental alignment of neural connectivity subgraphs to standardized anatomical coordinate scaffolds in computational neuroscience, Greedy placement of logic components onto available routing-adjacent sites in electronic design automation, Progressive registration of 3D scan keypoint neighborhoods onto template meshes in robotics and digital twins, Stepwise matching of molecular interaction substructures onto admissible binding-site topologies in computational chemistry

nodes.name: DerivePlacementFrontierInputs
nodes.description: Partition considered graph nodes into already-placed and currently-unplaced candidates to define the extension frontier.

inputs.name: considered_nodes
inputs.constraints: Order is preserved for initial candidate order.

inputs.name: mapping
inputs.constraints: Keys are currently placed graph nodes.

outputs.name: already_placed_nodes
outputs.constraints: Derived from mapping keys.

outputs.name: unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Subset of considered_nodes not in already_placed_nodes.

nodes.name: ComputeGreedyCandidateScores
nodes.description: Optionally invoke the greedy scoring heuristic for unplaced nodes and expose deterministic candidate scores for downstream ranking/reporting.

inputs.name: mapping_context
inputs.constraints: Provides graph, lattice stats, and previously generated subgraphs.

inputs.name: rank_nodes
inputs.constraints: When false, scoring can return empty maps.

outputs.name: ranking_scores
outputs.constraints: Primary score plus random tiebreaker, descending sort key.

outputs.name: candidate_scores
outputs.constraints: Primary heuristic score per candidate; empty when ranking is skipped.

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, unplaced_nodes: List[GraphNode], mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> tuple[Map[GraphNode,Tuple[float,float]], Map[GraphNode,float]]

nodes.name: PrioritizeUnplacedNodes
nodes.description: Choose candidate traversal order by sorting on heuristic ranking scores when enabled, otherwise keep original order.

inputs.name: ranking_scores
inputs.constraints: Used only when rank_nodes is true.

outputs.name: prioritized_unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Sorted descending by ranking_scores when rank_nodes=true.

nodes.type_signature: (unplaced_nodes: List[GraphNode], ranking_scores: Map[GraphNode,Tuple[float,float]], rank_nodes: bool) -> List[GraphNode]

nodes.name: GreedyAssignFrontierPlacements
nodes.description: Greedily iterate free lattice neighbors and assign the first candidate node that passes both mapped-lattice-neighbor and mapped-graph-neighbor edge-consistency checks.

inputs.name: mapping_context
inputs.constraints: Provides graph and lattice adjacency queries.

inputs.name: free_lattice_neighbors
inputs.constraints: Frontier lattice positions considered in iteration order.

inputs.name: unexpanded_nodes
inputs.constraints: Updated when a node is newly placed.

outputs.name: updated_mapping
outputs.constraints: Includes newly accepted placements.

outputs.name: updated_unmapping
outputs.constraints: Reverse map consistent with updated_mapping.

outputs.name: updated_unexpanded_nodes
outputs.constraints: Union of prior set and newly placed nodes.

outputs.name: updated_already_placed_nodes
outputs.constraints: Placement membership after greedy extension.

outputs.name: remaining_unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Candidates not placed after frontier sweep.

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, free_lattice_neighbors: Set[LatticeNode], prioritized_unplaced_nodes: List[GraphNode], already_placed_nodes: Set[GraphNode], mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode]) -> tuple[Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], Set[GraphNode], Set[GraphNode], List[GraphNode]]

nodes.name: BuildExtendedMappingStateWithPruningPolicy
nodes.description: Apply invalid-placement pruning policy to residual unplaced nodes and construct the final extended mapping state while carrying candidate scores.

inputs.name: mapping_context
inputs.constraints: Receives optional graph-node removals when pruning is enabled.

inputs.name: remaining_unplaced_nodes
inputs.constraints: Pruned from graph only when remove_invalid_placement_nodes=true.

outputs.name: extended_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Contains updated mapping/unmapping/frontier and pruning effects.

outputs.name: candidate_scores
outputs.constraints: Same semantic score map produced by scoring step.

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, updated_mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], updated_unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode], updated_unexpanded_nodes: Set[GraphNode], updated_already_placed_nodes: Set[GraphNode], remaining_unplaced_nodes: List[GraphNode], remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, candidate_scores: Map[GraphNode,float]) -> tuple[MappingState, Map[GraphNode,float]]

nodes.name: validate_current_mapping
nodes.description: Evaluate whether the current mapping/unmapping pair satisfies graph-lattice consistency constraints.

outputs.name: mapping_is_valid
outputs.constraints: true iff all structural constraints hold

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, mapping: Map[GraphNode,LatticeNode], unmapping: Map[LatticeNode,GraphNode]) -> bool
nodes.conceptual_summary: Bidirectional Relation Consistency Validator. Consumes a candidate pair of forward and reverse mappings between two discrete relational structures and tests whether they satisfy a shared constraint system. It verifies inverse coherence (round-trip consistency) and structural admissibility against topology/order constraints encoded in the context object. The result is a single feasibility predicate indicating whether the mapping pair is a valid structure-preserving correspondence.. Applications: Verifying correspondence between segmented regions in multimodal medical images under anatomical adjacency constraints, Checking consistency of symbol-to-state alignments in compiler intermediate-representation lowering with reverse traceability guarantees, Validating entity alignment between heterogeneous knowledge graphs while preserving ontology hierarchy relations, Confirming mesh-to-skeleton attachment maps in computational biomechanics with inverse lookup and topology constraints

nodes.name: RunGreedyMappingPipeline
nodes.description: Orchestrate initialization, greedy extension, and validity checking to produce a greedy UD subgraph rooted at the starting node.

outputs.name: generated_subgraph
outputs.constraints: greedy-generated UD subgraph

outputs.name: final_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: final immutable mapping/unmapping/frontier snapshot

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool, initialized_mapping_state: MappingState, extended_mapping_state: MappingState, mapping_is_valid: bool) -> tuple[Subgraph, MappingState]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Rooted Greedy Constrained Substructure Synthesis Pipeline. This operation composes three stages over a relational graph-like state space: seed-state initialization, heuristic greedy frontier expansion, and constraint validation. Starting from a designated root element, it incrementally adds locally best candidates (optionally ranked and pre-pruned) to build a connected partial structure. It then emits the constructed structure together with an immutable terminal snapshot of assignment/frontier partitions, conditioned by a validity predicate.. Applications: Growing a residue-interaction motif from a seed residue in a molecular contact graph under stereochemical constraints, Constructing a feasible restoration island from a seed bus in power-grid recovery planning with operational rule checks, Region-growing segmentation from a seed vertex in a 3D mesh using local similarity ranking and topology validity tests, Incremental assembly of a dependency-consistent module set from a seed component in software/build dependency graphs

nodes.name: InitializeSeedPlacement
nodes.description: Create empty bidirectional mapping state and place the starting graph node on its initial lattice node via initialization logic.

inputs.name: mapping_context
inputs.constraints: Provides graph, lattice, and initialize_mapping helper

inputs.name: starting_node
inputs.constraints: Must exist in the input graph

outputs.name: initialized_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Contains mapping, unmapping, and unexpanded_nodes seeded with starting_node

outputs.name: current_node
outputs.constraints: Initialized to starting_node

outputs.name: current_lattice_node
outputs.constraints: Lattice node assigned to current_node

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, starting_node: NodeId) -> tuple[MappingState, NodeId, NodeId]

nodes.name: RunGreedyFrontierExpansion
nodes.description: Iteratively consume unexpanded nodes, derive free lattice neighbors and graph neighbors, invoke greedy extension, and advance cursor until no frontier nodes remain.

inputs.name: mapping_context
inputs.constraints: Provides graph, lattice, and extend_mapping_with_nodes helper

inputs.name: initialized_mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Must contain a valid seed placement

inputs.name: current_lattice_node
inputs.constraints: Initial lattice cursor aligned with current_node

inputs.name: rank_nodes
inputs.constraints: Controls heuristic ranking during greedy placement

outputs.name: extended_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Frontier exhausted; mapping/unmapping updated after all greedy expansions

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_context: MappingContext, initialized_mapping_state: MappingState, current_node: NodeId, current_lattice_node: NodeId, remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> MappingState

nodes.name: EvaluateFinalMappingValidity
nodes.description: Check whether the final bidirectional mapping state is globally consistent.

inputs.name: extended_mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Candidate final state from greedy expansion

outputs.name: mapping_is_valid
outputs.constraints: True iff mapping and unmapping are mutually consistent and valid

nodes.name: BuildGreedySubgraphResult
nodes.description: Materialize pipeline outputs from the validated state, producing the greedy subgraph mapping and final state.

inputs.name: mapping_is_valid
inputs.constraints: Must be true to emit successful result

outputs.name: generated_subgraph
outputs.constraints: Derived from mapping in extended_mapping_state

outputs.name: final_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Returned terminal mapping state
